generatedBy: Phase L Postgres JDBC live test
note: Phase L integrated on top of Phase K. Score stays 149/189 (78.8%) but jdbc-catalog:v2's evidence column upgrades from 'crate compiles against fork' to 'live Postgres roundtrip'. Added postgres:16-alpine to docker-compose.test.yml; the jdbc_postgres_namespace_roundtrip test connects to localhost:15432, creates a namespace, lists it, drops it. Engine session manager dispatch refactor (gap #2 from Phase K) deferred to Phase M because it touches 60+ SessionCatalog usages across sqe-catalog + sqe-coordinator; that scope earns its own MR.

sqe:position-deletes:v3.notes: MoR DELETE on V3 tables writes position-delete files alongside the existing data files; live data file count stays unchanged after DELETE (no CoW rewrite). Verified with crates/sqe-coordinator/tests/v3_e2e.rs::position_deletes_on_v3_table against docker-compose.test.yml.

sqe:equality-deletes:v2.notes: DELETE with write.delete.mode=merge-on-read writes equality-delete files via EqualityDeleteFileWriter and commits through RowDeltaAction. Unit tests assert file structure (3 data + 2 pos-delete + 1 eq-delete + Operation::Overwrite). Cross-engine read verification (Spark 4.1) ships as #[ignore] integration test pending docker-compose.spark.yml.
caveats: No cross-engine read test executed (Spark interop is #[ignore]).

RowDeltaOperation::delete_entries simplified to Ok(vec![]) against the RisingWave fork's SnapshotProducer; behaviour matches the fork's own CoW path but not independently verified against Java Iceberg.

sqe:equality-deletes:v3.notes: Equality-delete UPDATE on a V3 table with a declared identifier-field-id commits a single RowDelta with the new data file and the equality-delete row, then the SELECT returns the post-update value. Verified with crates/sqe-coordinator/tests/v3_e2e.rs::equality_delete_update_on_v3_table.
caveats: Cross-engine read test against Spark still ships as #[ignore].

sqe:merge-on-read:v2.notes: All three DML kinds (DELETE, UPDATE, MERGE) route through RowDeltaAction when write.*.mode = merge-on-read. UPDATE emits one data file per batch of matched rows plus an equality delete for old PKs. MERGE composes MATCHED UPDATE (data file + equality delete), MATCHED DELETE (equality delete only), and NOT MATCHED INSERT (data file only) into one RowDelta commit. Without a declared PK the dispatcher falls back to CoW for UPDATE and MERGE; DELETE falls back to position deletes.
caveats: Cross-engine read test (Spark 4.1, Trino 465) ships as #[ignore] pending docker-compose.spark.yml / docker-compose.trino.yml.
MoR benchmark harness at scripts/benchmark-mor-vs-cow.sh is scripted; live timings require the test stack.

sqe:merge-on-read:v3.notes: MoR DELETE writes position-delete files; MoR UPDATE writes data + equality-delete in one RowDelta when the V3 table declares an identifier-field-id. Both paths verified end-to-end against Polaris.
caveats: Cross-engine read test against Spark still ships as #[ignore].

sqe:copy-on-write:v2.notes: DELETE/UPDATE/MERGE via RisingWave iceberg-rust fork rewrite_files().

sqe:copy-on-write:v3.notes: DELETE on a V3 table without a declared MoR property runs CoW: rewrites the matched files via RewriteFilesAction, drops the row from subsequent SELECTs, and commits a fresh snapshot through Polaris.

sqe:schema-evolution:v3.notes: ADD COLUMN with DEFAULT lands the new field with both write_default and initial_default on a V3 table; the column appears in information_schema.columns after the commit. Verified end-to-end against Polaris.
caveats: iceberg-rust's TableScan reads the snapshot's declared schema rather than metadata.current_schema(), so retroactive initial_default fill on pre-evolution snapshots is not surfaced yet (read-side gap, not write-side).

sqe:type-promotion:v3.notes: ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... SET DATA TYPE widens columns on V3 tables (verified int -> bigint). The new type surfaces through information_schema.columns and the schema change commits through Polaris.
caveats: Nanosecond-to-microsecond narrowing not attempted (Iceberg spec rejects narrowing).

sqe:column-default-values:v3.notes: CREATE TABLE ... DEFAULT <literal> applies write_default; ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... DEFAULT applies initial_default. Function-call defaults rejected with clear error.

sqe:table-creation:v3.notes: CREATE TABLE auto-upgrades to format-version 3 when columns require V3 features (TIMESTAMP_NS, DEFAULT). Polaris materialises V3 metadata because SQE forwards format-version through the reserved table property (Iceberg REST has no dedicated format-version field on CreateTableRequest).

sqe:time-travel:v2.notes: FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF + 6 metadata TVFs (Step 8c).

sqe:time-travel:v3.notes: FOR VERSION AS OF works end-to-end on V3 tables: the classifier strips the clause, apply_version_spec registers a snapshot-pinned provider under datafusion.public.<alias>, and the SQL is rewritten to reference it. SELECT against the pinned snapshot returns the historical row count.

sqe:table-maintenance:v2.notes: CALL system.expire_snapshots / remove_orphan_files / rewrite_manifests wrap vendored actions. rewrite_data_files currently consolidates manifests only (no Parquet re-encoding); full compaction lands as a Phase B follow-up.
caveats: rewrite_data_files does not re-encode Parquet payloads.

sqe:table-maintenance:v3.notes: CALL system.rewrite_data_files merges files on V3 tables; row count preserved, file count drops, snapshot log moves forward. Same maintenance code path as V2 with the same Parquet-re-encoding caveat.
caveats: rewrite_data_files does not re-encode Parquet payloads (row groups stay as-is).

sqe:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Transaction::{create_branch, create_tag, drop_branch, drop_tag} in vendored iceberg-rust + ALTER TABLE ... CREATE BRANCH/TAG DDL + SET WRITE_BRANCH session var. Main branch drop rejected.

sqe:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Same transaction actions apply to V3 tables; ref semantics are format-version agnostic.

sqe:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Read path honours all V2 transforms (year/month/day/hour/bucket/truncate/identity); partition columns surface through the scanner and partition pruning is applied. Write path lacks a SQL `PARTITIONED BY (...)` clause: tables are created via Spark/Trino and read by SQE.
caveats: CREATE TABLE in SQE has no partition-spec syntax yet; iceberg-rust UnboundPartitionSpec is plumbed but unwired in the SQL handler.

sqe:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Same iceberg-rust scan path as V2 reads V3-format partition specs; transforms over V3 types (TIMESTAMP_NS) decode through the same Transform machinery. Write path shares the V2 gap: CREATE TABLE has no PARTITIONED BY syntax in SQE yet.
caveats: Same write-path gap as V2: no SQL syntax for partition spec at create time.

sqe:partition-evolution:v2.notes: Read path tolerates partition spec evolution: iceberg-rust returns the per-data-file spec id and the scanner uses it to pick the right transforms when reading. Write-side spec evolution (ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP/REPLACE PARTITION FIELD) is not wired into SQE's DDL handler yet.
caveats: ALTER TABLE for partition spec evolution not implemented in SQE.

sqe:partition-evolution:v3.notes: V3 metadata stores per-file partition_spec_id the same way V2 does; the scan path picks up evolved specs identically. Write-side spec evolution shares the V2 gap.
caveats: Same write-path gap as V2.

sqe:read-support:v3.notes: SELECT against V3 tables works through the existing iceberg-rust scan path: TIMESTAMP_NS columns materialise as Arrow Timestamp(Nanosecond), INSERT/SELECT round-trips, COUNT(*) and projections all return the expected results.
caveats: Variant/geometry not supported (separate cells track those gaps).

sqe:write-insert:v3.notes: INSERT into V3 tables with TIMESTAMP_NS columns commits a snapshot through Polaris and the rows materialise on subsequent SELECT. INSERT round-trip verified end-to-end.
caveats: Variant/geometry writes not supported (separate cells track those gaps).

sqe:write-merge-update-delete:v2.notes: All three DML ops via CoW rewrite_files().

sqe:write-merge-update-delete:v3.notes: DELETE (CoW + MoR position), UPDATE (MoR equality), and MERGE INTO (MATCHED UPDATE + NOT MATCHED INSERT) all commit on V3 tables. Each path has a direct V3 e2e test asserting the post-state through the catalog.

sqe:catalog-integration:v3.notes: V3 tables created by SQE materialise correctly in the Iceberg REST catalog: CREATE/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE all commit, table metadata reloads carry the right format-version, and Polaris accepts V3-only column types because format-version is forwarded as a table property.

sqe:statistics:v2.notes: Column metrics used for pruning and CBO.

sqe:statistics:v3.notes: SHOW STATS FOR works on V3 tables, returning per-column metrics from the active snapshot. Same DataFusion + iceberg-rust scan path as V2; V3 metadata participates without modification.

sqe:bloom-filters:v2.notes: All current SQE write paths (coordinator CTAS, streaming INSERT, MERGE, rewrite_data_files) build WriterProperties via the shared parquet_writer_config helper, which honours write.parquet.bloom-filter-columns and write.parquet.bloom-filter-fpp. DataFusion reads blooms automatically. Puffin theta-sketch sidecar emitted via puffin_stats. CALL system.suggest_bloom_filter_columns analyses query history. SQE has no separate worker-side data writer today; if/when distributed writes land, they will use the same helper.
caveats: DataFusion StatisticsSource consumer for Puffin NDV is blocked on DF 54 (apache/datafusion#21157).
End-to-end read-probe assertion (parquet footer inspection) lives in the bloom suite tests; not asserted in v3_e2e.

sqe:bloom-filters:v3.notes: V3 tables accept the bloom-filter property at CREATE TIME, the property survives the round-trip through Polaris, and SHOW CREATE TABLE re-emits it (verified in the V3 e2e suite). Writer path is format-version agnostic and uses the same parquet_writer_config helper as V2.
caveats: Same DF 54 NDV consumer block as V2 (apache/datafusion#21157).
Read-probe footer inspection asserted in the bloom suite, not v3_e2e.

sqe:hive-metastore:v2.notes: iceberg-catalog-hms vendored from apache/iceberg-rust v0.9.0 into vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/hms/, wired behind `cargo --features hms`. The RisingWave fork's CatalogBuilder trait gained a default `with_storage_factory` so the upstream HMS code compiles unmodified except for a small FileIOBuilder patch (fork takes a scheme string instead of a StorageFactory). The `HmsBackend::build_catalog` wrapper builds a working `HmsCatalog`. Default REST-only builds do NOT pull in volo-thrift / pilota / hive_metastore.
caveats: No live integration test against an HMS server yet (docker-compose.hms.yml not in repo).
Engine-level multi-backend session refactor still pending: a coordinator built with --features hms can construct an HmsCatalog but the engine's session manager still hardcodes the REST path.

sqe:hive-metastore:v3.notes: Same vendored iceberg-catalog-hms code path as V2; the format-version property forwarding proven on Polaris applies identically. HMS catalog itself is format-version agnostic.
caveats: No live integration test at V3 either.
Same engine-wiring gap as V2.

sqe:aws-glue-catalog:v2.notes: iceberg-catalog-glue vendored from apache/iceberg-rust v0.9.0 into vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/glue/, wired behind `cargo --features glue`. FileIOBuilder patched the same way as HMS to use the fork's scheme-string API. The `GlueBackend::build_catalog` wrapper builds a working `GlueCatalog`. Default REST-only builds do NOT pull in aws-sdk-glue or aws-config.
caveats: No live integration test against AWS Glue (would need real AWS credentials or LocalStack).
Same engine-wiring gap as HMS: session manager hardcodes REST.

sqe:aws-glue-catalog:v3.notes: Same vendored iceberg-catalog-glue code path as V2; format-version agnostic.
caveats: No live integration test at V3 either.
Same engine-wiring gap as V2.

sqe:rest-catalog:v3.notes: Iceberg REST catalogs accept V3 tables when SQE forwards format-version as a table property in CreateTableRequest. Verified end-to-end against Polaris (Apache Polaris is REST-spec compliant).

sqe:nessie:v2.notes: Works via iceberg-catalog-rest against Nessie's REST adapter; documented in docs/deployment.md Section 5b.
caveats: Write parity depends on Nessie's REST adapter completeness.

sqe:nessie:v3.notes: Nessie speaks the Iceberg REST protocol; SQE's REST catalog now forwards format-version: 3 through the reserved table property, which Nessie's REST adapter respects. Same code path that proved on Polaris. No live integration test against Nessie yet.
caveats: No live test against Nessie's REST adapter.

sqe:polaris:v3.notes: Apache Polaris 1.3.0-incubating round-trips V3 tables when SQE sets format-version in the CreateTableRequest properties map. CREATE/INSERT/SELECT/DELETE/UPDATE all verified against the test stack.

sqe:unity-catalog:v2.notes: Compatible via REST; OIDC M2M auth provider landed in Phase A.
caveats: No live integration test against Unity Catalog yet.

sqe:unity-catalog:v3.notes: Unity Catalog exposes an Iceberg REST adapter; format-version: 3 is forwarded through the reserved table property by SQE's REST writer. The OIDC M2M auth provider (Phase A) authenticates against Unity. No live test against Unity yet.
caveats: No live integration test against Unity Catalog.

sqe:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2.notes: Horizon is Polaris-based; REST wiring compatible; no live test.
caveats: No live integration test.

sqe:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v3.notes: Snowflake Horizon is Polaris-based; the REST surface is shared with Polaris which we verified end-to-end at format-version 3. No live test against a Horizon endpoint.
caveats: No live integration test against Horizon.

sqe:hadoop-catalog:v2.notes: Storage-only scanner walks warehouse paths for metadata/v*.metadata.json (feature `hadoop`). Read path only; writes defer to REST/HMS.
caveats: Write path is race-prone on object stores without atomic rename; intentionally read-oriented.

sqe:hadoop-catalog:v3.notes: The hadoop-catalog scanner walks `metadata/v*.metadata.json`; format-version 1/2/3 are read by the same iceberg-rust deserializer. Read parity with V2 holds. Write path remains intentionally read-oriented (race-prone on object stores).
caveats: Read-only on V3 too; writes still defer to REST/HMS.

sqe:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: Two paths ship: (1) the rusqlite-based SQLite helper in backends/sql.rs (always-available with `--features sql`), and (2) iceberg-catalog-sql vendored from apache/iceberg-rust v0.9.0 (vendor/iceberg-rust/crates/catalog/sql/) using sqlx::any to dispatch to SQLite/PostgreSQL/MySQL. Postgres path verified end-to-end with `jdbc_postgres_namespace_roundtrip` in crates/sqe-catalog/tests/backends_integration.rs against the docker-compose.test.yml postgres service: create_namespace + list_namespaces + drop_namespace all succeed against a live Postgres instance.
caveats: Engine session manager does not yet consume iceberg-catalog-sql; the rusqlite helper is the only path the coordinator routes to today.
Live test exercises namespace ops; create_table needs a writable warehouse FileIO and was not included in the Phase L slice.

sqe:jdbc-catalog:v3.notes: iceberg-catalog-sql vendored alongside the fork is format-version agnostic; the format-version: 3 property forwarding pattern proven on Polaris works identically here. SQLite, PostgreSQL, and MySQL all accept V3 metadata.
caveats: No live test against a JDBC catalog at V3 yet.

sqe:variant-type:v3.notes: Deferred to next cycle. iceberg-rust #2188 not merged.

sqe:vector-type:v3.notes: Deferred. Iceberg V3 vector spec not finalised.

sqe:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: TIMESTAMP_NS(N) and TIMESTAMPTZ_NS(N) DDL route to PrimitiveType::TimestampNs/TimestamptzNs; Arrow bridge and INSERT/SELECT wiring covered by v3_types_integration.

sqe:cdc-support:v3.notes: FOR INCREMENTAL BETWEEN SNAPSHOT works end-to-end on V3 tables: pre-classification strips the clause, the planner registers an IncrementalTableProvider, and the scan returns the rows added in the requested range. Verified against Polaris.
caveats: Snapshot id ordering: the validator currently uses numeric comparison (start > end) rather than parent-chain ancestry. Random Iceberg snapshot ids can fall in either order; the start = 0 sentinel sidesteps the issue. Tracked separately.
